summary: Belgium's posture is led by the Centre for Cybersecurity Belgium, which urges organisations to prepare for the quantum era and frames its advice around the European Union's strategic roadmap milestones. Acting as the national coordination centre, the CCB also channels EU cybersecurity funding towards post-quantum cryptography topics, including the transition to post-quantum public key infrastructures. The federal public service BOSA points organisations to the NIST 2024 standards, and the National Bank of Belgium tracks post-quantum cryptography as an emerging risk for financial market infrastructures. No Belgian national migration deadline or sovereign algorithm list has been published.
processParticipation: NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap
migrationTimeline:
2030 | High-risk use cases migrated
2035 | Full migration of all systems complete
govActors:
CCB | Centre for Cybersecurity Belgium, national cyber security authority and national coordination centre (NCC-BE) channelling EU PQC funding
BOSA | Federal public service for policy and support, quantum cybersecurity guidance
NBB | National Bank of Belgium, financial market infrastructure oversight
mainRegulation:
NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555, Art. 21(2)(h) | EU | binding-law
DORA, Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 (financial sector) | EU | binding-law
Commission Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 on a coordinated PQC roadmap | EU | soft-law
NIS Cooperation Group Coordinated Implementation Roadmap (2026/2030/2035) | EU | soft-law
